# Qwen3-TTS (pure-C, CPU) — API reference for LLM agents

Self-hostable text-to-speech HTTP service. Pure C inference engine for Alibaba's
Qwen3-TTS models (0.6B / 1.7B), CPU-only, no Python/PyTorch. OpenAI-compatible
endpoint included. Docker image: `ghcr.io/bon5co/qwen3-tts:latest` (linux/amd64 + arm64).

## Endpoints

Base URL: `http://<host>:<PORT>` (default port 8080).

### POST /v1/tts — synthesize speech, returns audio/wav
Request JSON:
{
  "text": "Hello world",            // required; supports inline emotion markup: "[joy] Great! [sad] Bye."
  "speaker": "ryan",                 // optional; see GET /v1/speakers (default: ryan)
  "language": "English",             // optional
  "emotion": "joy",                  // optional: joy|sad|angry|fear|disgust|surprise|neutral
  "instruct": "whisper softly",      // optional free-form style instruction
  "volume": 1.0,                     // optional gain multiplier
  "rate": 1.0                        // optional speaking-rate multiplier
}
Response: WAV bytes (16-bit PCM mono 24000 Hz).

### POST /v1/tts/stream — same body, returns chunked raw PCM (s16le mono 24 kHz)
First audio arrives before synthesis completes (low TTFA).

### POST /v1/audio/speech — OpenAI-compatible
Request JSON: { "input": "text to speak", "voice": "ryan", "model": "ignored" }
Response: WAV bytes. Drop-in replacement for OpenAI's /v1/audio/speech.

### GET /v1/speakers — JSON list of preset speakers
### GET /v1/health — returns 200 when ready (use for readiness probes)

## Example

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/v1/tts \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"text":"Hello from Qwen TTS.","speaker":"ryan"}' -o out.wav

## Container environment variables

MODEL       large | small | voice-design | base-small | base-large  (default: large = 1.7B)
PORT        HTTP port (default 8080)
WORKERS     concurrent synthesis workers (default 4)
BATCH_SIZE  request batching, >=2 enables vLLM-style batching (default 1)
QUANT       int8 | int4 | bf16 (default int8; int4 is 1.7B-only)
MODELS_ROOT model storage dir (default /models — mount a persistent volume here)

## Operational notes

- First boot downloads model weights from HuggingFace into $MODELS_ROOT
  (~1.4 GB small, ~3.4 GB large). Mount a volume at /models to avoid re-downloading.
- RAM: ~3 GB (0.6B int8), ~4-6 GB (1.7B int8). CPU-bound; more vCPUs = lower latency.
- Synthesis is slower than realtime on typical cloud vCPUs (RTF ~2 on 8 shared vCPU
  for 0.6B). Use /v1/tts/stream for lower perceived latency.
- Health endpoint returns 200 only after model weights are loaded.

## Source layout (for code navigation)

- qwen_tts_server.c — HTTP server + JSON parsing (all endpoints above)
- qwen_tts.c / qwen_tts_talker.c / qwen_tts_code_predictor.c / qwen_tts_speech_decoder.c — inference pipeline
- qwen_tts_kernels*.c — SIMD matmul kernels (NEON/AVX2/AVX-512, int8 SDOT/VNNI)
- main.c — CLI entry; `--serve <port>` starts the server
- Dockerfile / docker-entrypoint.sh — container build + first-boot model download
- download_model.sh — HuggingFace weight fetcher (invoked by entrypoint)
